title: arisarum vulgare var. clusii
intro: Arisarum vulgare var. clusii, also known as the European birthwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Arum, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the roots of Arisarum vulgare var. clusii are typically used in the form of decoctions, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat a range of health issues.
